symptoms.symptom_name: Wet gurgling cough beginning immediately on feeding
symptoms.onset: at the moment of the pureed lunch

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A cough triggered at the instant of swallowing in a patient with dysphagia is the defining history for aspiration.
Systemic infective response consistent with a lower respiratory tract infection.
High fever with purulent sputum is unusual for PE and favours infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypoxaemia (SpO2 89% RA) with tachypnoea and accessory-muscle use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective respiratory compromise; a caregiver-obtainable oximeter reading admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Purulent discoloured sputum with marked halitosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Foul-smelling breath and purulent sputum point to a mixed anaerobic infection typical of aspiration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known dysphagia with recurrent feeding difficulty and recent weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An unsafe swallow is the mechanistic risk factor that makes aspiration the leading diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fed near-supine with head of bed flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Supine feeding removes gravity protection of the airway and is a modifiable contributor.
Caregiver-reported facility cuff/monitor readings indicating emerging sepsis; unverified home-grade data but strongly reinforces urgent escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Focal right-lower-lobe coarse crackles, bronchial breath sounds and dullness to percussion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal consolidation signs in the dependent right lower lobe — a clinician auscultation/percussion finding, sealed as it cannot be obtained in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent gag reflex / profound bulbar weakness on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the unsafe-swallow mechanism; a neurological examination finding not obtainable over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Right lower lobe infiltrate on chest radiograph

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis remains presumptive; severity and complications (effusion, abscess) undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiographic confirmation of the consolidation — a hospital investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of a feeding-triggered wet cough, fever, hypoxaemia and purulent sputum in a patient with known dysphagia is a coherent, high-confidence picture of aspiration pneumonia mandating emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department assessment with chest radiograph, bloods (FBC, CRP, lactate, cultures) and blood gas
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms diagnosis, quantifies severity and sepsis, and enables oxygen and IV anaerobe-covering antibiotics — all requiring hospital-level care.
Once stabilised, formally assesses swallow safety to guide diet modification and prevent recurrence — not an acute-phase test.
